rulings.Case Title: IN RE: PIER 39 LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 1, 2023, line 1. Respondent's Motion for Withdrawal of Attorney of Record is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MHYF HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. CHRIS V. KELLY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: CROSS-DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PRAYER FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 1, 2023 line 5. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PRAYER FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES is GRANTED with leave to amend to allege facts giving rise to recover of attorney's fees incurred by the cross-complainant in defending the complaint or prosecuting the cross-complaint. To the extent the cross-complaint seeks recovery of attorney's fees by the Plaintiff, the motion is moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MHYF HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. CHRIS V. KELLY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 1, 2023 line 5. CROSS DEFENDANT SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY, INC., NEILL BASSI DEMURRER to 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for the cross-complainant to allege facts (not conclusions) giving rise to the cross-defendant's liability not covered by the indemnity clause. Gardner v. Murphy (1975) 54 Cal.App.3d 164; Baird v. Jones (1993) 21 Cal.App.4th 684; Hollywood Turf Club v. Montgomery Elevator Co. (1976) 58 Cal.App.3d 580. Cross-Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SALLY L. LIU VS. FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 1, 2023 line 8. DEFENDANT BAYMARK FINANCIAL, INC., MARK ENGELMAN, VERA NOVOSEL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry their burden and show that the entire complaint or any of the claims upon which relief may be granted is asserted against them in the complaint arise out of protected activity (service of notice). The Court notes that analysis of each cause of action is limited to 2-5 lines, for example the claims seeking cancellation of the Deed of Trust, Notice of Default and Notice of Trustee's Sale is asserted to arise out of protected activity (service of 3 Day Notice) as follows: "The causes of cause of action for "cancellation of instruments" is based, at least in part, on petitioning activity. It incorporates by reference all 69 paragraphs that precede it." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
